_comment: Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/Chalazion Removal/ : Chalazion Removal_Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + Chalazion Removal_PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Diagnosis is backed by clinical signs, symptoms, and ophthalmic examination
Does not respond to conservative medical therapy

claim.label: Intraoperative photograph with time and date stamp

ppd_preauth.q: Did the clinical examination suggest presence of chalazion with following symptoms small, red inflamed area of the eyelids?
Detailed Clinical notes, clinical symptoms and visual acuity?
Clinical photo of Affected part with Proper labelling of Affected Eye whether R or L with full face photograph.

cpd_claim.q: Do OT notes detail the steps of surgery performed and was the surgery successful?
Are the documents available to show appropriate post-op care, advise including for follow-up?
Was the intra operative photograph submitted?
Is the document evidence present for the specimen being send for the Histopathology examination?

common_queries: Clinical examination did not suggest presence of chalazion with symptoms of small, red inflamed area of the eyelids.
Clinical notes are not detailed, or do not describe clinical symptoms and visual acuity.
Clinical photograph of affected part is missing or not properly labelled with R or L eye, or full face photograph is missing.
Operative/procedure notes are missing or do not detail the steps of surgery performed or surgical outcome.
Documents showing appropriate post-op care and follow-up advice are not available.
Intraoperative photograph with time and date stamp is missing.
Histopathology report is missing or does not show evidence of specimen being sent for examination (if lesion atypical).

clinical_key_pointers: Surgical specimen should be sent for histopathology if lesion is atypical.
Early stage Chalazion small, red inflamed area of the eyelids
After few days the inflammation can develop into painless and slow growing lump
A chalazion can appear on the upper or lower eyelid
